perturbation.original_text: NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 4/6 - to safeguard Confidential Information as it does for its own proprietary information of like importance, but at least with a reasonable degree of care, - to use Confidential Information only for the Purpose and to divulge Confidential Information to its personnel for said Purpose only and on a "need to know" basis, - not to duplicate or otherwise reproduce Confidential Information except for such copies as the Receiving Party may require for the Purpose as aforesaid, provided that all copies shall contain the same proprietary and confidential notices and legends as appear on the original Confidential Information, - not to divulge Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, unless and until expressly authorized in writing to do so by the Disclosing Party, - not to reverse-compile, reverse-assemble or reverse-engineer Confidential Information or any part thereof, unless directed to do so by the disclosing Party as proven by appropriate documentation - not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on Confidential Information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 4/6 - to safeguard Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care, - to use Confidential Information only for the Purpose and to divulge Confidential Information to its personnel for said Purpose only and on a "need to know" basis, - not to duplicate or otherwise reproduce Confidential Information except for such copies as the Receiving Party may require for the Purpose as aforesaid, provided that all copies shall contain the same proprietary and confidential notices and legends as appear on the original Confidential Information, - not to divulge Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, unless and until expressly authorized in writing to do so by the Disclosing Party, - not to reverse-compile, reverse-assemble or reverse-engineer Confidential Information or any part thereof, unless directed to do so by the disclosing Party as proven by appropriate documentation - not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on Confidential Information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the clause "as it does for its own proprietary information of like importance" which creates a conflict. By removing this clause, the level of care is now undefined which could be interpreted as a lesser degree of protection compared to the standard for its own proprietary information. This contradicts the original intent of safeguarding Confidential Information with similar importance. It now introduces ambiguity regarding the required level of care and enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: - The Receiving Party undertakes during the term of this Agreement and for a period of five (5) years after the date of its expiry or termination: NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 4/6 - to safeguard Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care, - to use Confidential Information only for the Purpose and to divulge Confidential Information to its personnel for said Purpose only and on a "need to know" basis, - not to duplicate or otherwise reproduce Confidential Information except for such copies as the Receiving Party may require for the Purpose as aforesaid, provided that all copies shall contain the same proprietary and confidential notices and legends as appear on the original Confidential Information, - not to divulge Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, unless and until expressly authorized in writing to do so by the Disclosing Party, - not to reverse-compile, reverse-assemble or reverse-engineer Confidential Information or any part thereof, unless directed to do so by the disclosing Party as proven by appropriate documentation - not to claim nor to register any Intellectual Property right, nor to exercise any Intellectual Property right or any other right on Confidential Information received under the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies safeguarding Confidential Information 'as it does for its own proprietary information of like importance'. The changed text removes this comparator, leaving only 'a reasonable degree of care'. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because 'a reasonable degree of care' is now undefined relative to the party's own confidential information, which could mean a lesser standard is acceptable. Since the Contradicted Text also states '- to safeguard Confidential Information with a reasonable degree of care', the altered definition introduces ambiguity that can cause disagreement to how the Confidential Information should be handled, breaking enforcement of required 'reasonable degree of care'. This qualifies as an inconsistency leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5 - Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring upon the Receiving Party, whether expressly or impliedly, any right by license or otherwise under any proprietary or statutory right of the Disclosing Party existing prior to or coming into existence after the Effective Date of the Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5 - Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring upon the Receiving Party, whether expressly or impliedly, any right by license or otherwise under any proprietary or statutory right of the Disclosing Party existing prior to or coming into existence after the Effective Date of the Agreement. However, the Receiving party will have a limited license to use the IP to fulfill the purpose.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds "However, the Receiving party will have a limited license to use the IP to fulfill the purpose." which directly contradicts the initial statement in Section 5. Initially, no rights are granted, but now a limited license is introduced. This creates an in-text contradiction concerning whether the Receiving Party has any rights to the Disclosing Party's IP, creating enforcement uncertainty about the scope of IP use allowed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5 - Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring upon the Receiving Party, whether expressly or impliedly, any right by license or otherwise under any proprietary or statutory right of the Disclosing Party existing prior to or coming into existence after the Effective Date of the Agreement. However, the Receiving party will have a limited license to use the IP to fulfill the purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5 explicitly states that the Receiving Party receives no rights to the Disclosing Party's IP. The changed text adds a clause granting a limited license to use the IP, directly contradicting the initial statement within the same section. The contradictory text is found in the same location as the changed text, further solidifying the presence of a direct in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7 - The Confidential Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to Confidential Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Confidential Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7 - The Confidential Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to Confidential Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Confidential Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party. However, the disclosing party warrants that they have the right to disclose the Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "However, the disclosing party warrants that they have the right to disclose the Confidential Information." introduces an in-text contradiction. The section initially disclaims all warranties regarding the Confidential Information. By adding this warranty, there is now conflicting information about whether the Disclosing Party makes any warranties, creating uncertainty over liability and enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7 - The Confidential Information is disclosed, “as is”. The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties, whether express or implied, with respect to Confidential Information, and notably their fitness for a commercial technical purpose. The use of Confidential Information is made at the sole risk of the Receiving Party. However, the disclosing party warrants that they have the right to disclose the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that 'The Disclosing Party makes no representations or warranties...'. The changed text adds 'However, the disclosing party warrants that they have the right to disclose the Confidential Information.'. This directly contradicts the previous statement of 'no warranties', creating an inconsistency as the same party is both disclaiming and giving a warranty within the same section, satisfying the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' definition, which states that in-text contradictions happen when different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties.

perturbation.original_text: 8 - The Agreement is personal to the Parties (« intuitu personae ») and the Receiving Party undertakes not to assign nor transfer its rights or obligations under the Agreement to any third party, including an affiliated company, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written approval.
perturbation.changed_text: 8 - The Agreement is personal to the Parties (« intuitu personae ») and the Receiving Party undertakes not to assign nor transfer its rights or obligations under the Agreement to any third party, including an affiliated company, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written approval. Notwithstanding the previous sentence, the Receiving Party may assign the contract to a subsidary without the prior written approval.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of "Notwithstanding the previous sentence, the Receiving Party may assign the contract to a subsidary without the prior written approval." introduces an in-text contradiction. First the text says the contract may not be assigned, then it says it may be assigned to a subsidary, creating an enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8 - The Agreement is personal to the Parties (« intuitu personae ») and the Receiving Party undertakes not to assign nor transfer its rights or obligations under the Agreement to any third party, including an affiliated company, without the Disclosing Party’s prior written approval. Notwithstanding the previous sentence, the Receiving Party may assign the contract to a subsidary without the prior written approval.
perturbation.justification: The original text in section 8 prohibits assignment or transfer of rights/obligations to any third party without prior written approval. The changed text introduces an exception, allowing assignment to a subsidiary without approval. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition, creating an in-text contradiction as described in the explanation and as an example of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting... obligations'. Since the Changed Text and Contradicted text are in the same location, this doesn't mean that no contradiction was produced.

perturbation.original_text: 9 - Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one arbitrator appointed in accordance with those Rules. The arbitration proceedings shall take place in Paris (France). The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 5/6 The applicable law shall be Belgian Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: 9 - Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one arbitrator appointed in accordance with those Rules. The arbitration proceedings shall take place in Paris (France). The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 5/6 The applicable law shall be French Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The change modifies the applicable law from Belgian Law to French Law. This creates an in-text contradiction because the contract refers that Belgian Law will be used, when in this change is being set to French Law. The enforcement uncertainty refers to which law should be applied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9 - Any dispute arising in connection with this Agreement, including any dispute related to the validity, interpretation, implementation and/or termination of the Agreement, which cannot be amicably settled within the sixty (60) days following its occurrence shall be finally settled under the Rules of Arbitration of the International Chamber of Commerce by one arbitrator appointed in accordance with those Rules. The arbitration proceedings shall take place in Paris (France). The language to be used in the arbitral proceedings shall be English. NDA – EUNCL & Sponsor 5/6 The applicable law shall be French Law, under which the dispute, controversy or claim referred to arbitration shall be decided without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.justification: The change modifies the applicable law from Belgian Law to French Law in Section 9. Since the contradicted text is in the same location (Section 9), this creates a direct and irreconcilable in-text contradiction regarding which law should govern disputes, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability. The Perturbation Explanation is correct, because the contract explicitly states that Belgian Law will be used, and then this change sets it to French Law.
